title: CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
description: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct ensuring a harassment-free community.
content:
# Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct

## Our Pledge

We as members, contributors, and leaders pledge to make participation in our
community a harassment-free experience for everyone, regardless of age, body
size, visible or invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender
identity and expression, level of experience, education, socio-economic status,
nationality, personal appearance, race, religion, or sexual identity
and orientation.

We pledge to act and interact in ways that contribute to an open, welcoming,
diverse, inclusive, and healthy community.

## Our Standards

Examples of behavior that contributes to a positive environment:
* Demonstrating empathy and kindness toward other people
* Being respectful of differing opinions, viewpoints, and experiences
* Giving and gracefully accepting constructive feedback
* Accepting responsibility and apologizing to those affected by our mistakes

Examples of unacceptable behavior:
* The use of sexualized language or imagery, and sexual attention or advances
* Trolling, insulting or derogatory comments, and personal or political attacks
* Public or private harassment
* Publishing others' private information without explicit permission

## Enforcement Responsibilities

Community leaders are responsible for clarifying and enforcing our standards.

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies within all community spaces.

## Enforcement

Instances of abusive, harassing, or otherwise unacceptable behavior may be
reported to the community leaders responsible for enforcement.

## Attribution

This Code of Conduct is adapted from the Contributor Covenant, version 2.0.

title: SECURITY.md
description: Security policy template for reporting vulnerabilities responsibly.
content:
# Security Policy

## Supported Versions

| Version | Supported |
| ------- | --------- |
| 2.x     | Yes |
| 1.x     | Yes |
| < 1.0   | No |

## Reporting a Vulnerability

We take security seriously. If you believe you have found a security
vulnerability, please report it to us via email at security@example.com.
Please do NOT report security vulnerabilities through public GitHub issues.

You should receive a response within 48 hours. Please include:
- Type of issue (e.g. SQL injection, XSS, etc.)
- Full paths of source file(s) related to the issue
- Step-by-step instructions to reproduce
- Proof-of-concept or exploit code (if available)

## Disclosure Policy

We follow a coordinated disclosure process:
1. Security report is received and assigned a handler
2. Problem is confirmed and affected versions identified
3. Fixes are prepared for all supported versions
4. Fixes are released and vulnerability announced

title: SUPPORT.md
description: Support document guiding users to appropriate help channels and resources.
content:
# Support

Thank you for using this project! Here's how to get help.

## Documentation
- README.md - Quick start and installation
- FAQ.md - Frequently asked questions
- CONTRIBUTING.md - How to contribute

## Getting Help

### 1. GitHub Discussions
Use GitHub Discussions for questions, feature ideas, and show-and-tell.

### 2. Issue Tracker
Open a GitHub Issue for bug reports and feature requests.

### 3. Community Chat
Join our community chat on Discord or Slack.

## How to Ask a Good Question
1. Search first - your question may already be answered
2. Be specific - include what you're trying to do
3. Include details - OS, version, code snippets, error messages
4. Show what you've tried

title: CODEOWNERS
description: GitHub CODEOWNERS file defining code review responsibilities per file pattern.
content:
# CODEOWNERS
#
# This file defines individuals or teams responsible for code review.
# When a PR modifies owned code, the listed owner is auto-added as reviewer.
#
# Learn more: https://docs.github.com/repositories/managing-your-repositorys-settings-and-features/customizing-your-repository/about-code-owners

# Global owners
* @trusted-maintainer

# Core application code
/src/ @core-team @senior-dev

# API layer
/api/ @api-maintainer

# Documentation
*.md @docs-team
README.md @project-lead

# Configuration files
*.yml @devops-team
Dockerfile @devops-team

# CI/CD
.github/workflows/ @devops-team

# Tests
/tests/ @qa-team

# Database migrations
/migrations/ @backend-team

# Frontend specific
*.css @frontend-team
*.jsx @frontend-team

# Security sensitive
*.pem @security-team
*.key @security-team

title: CHANGELOG.md
description: Keep a changelog following the Keep a Changelog convention with semantic versioning.
content:
# Changelog

All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

## [Unreleased]

### Added
- New feature X that provides Y capability

### Changed
- Updated dependency from version 1.0 to 2.0

### Fixed
- Corrected memory leak in WebSocket handler (#123)

### Security
- Patched XSS vulnerability in user profile rendering (#130)

## [1.1.0] - 2026-03-15

### Added
- User preference center for theme and notification settings
- Rate limiting on public API endpoints

### Changed
- Improved query performance on dashboard by 40%

### Fixed
- Resolved CSRF token regeneration on page refresh
- Fixed incorrect timezone handling in scheduled tasks

## [1.0.0] - 2025-12-01

### Added
- Initial release with authentication, CRUD, and search
- 100+ unit tests achieving 92% code coverage

title: FAQ.md
description: Frequently Asked Questions covering common setup, usage, and troubleshooting scenarios.
content:
# Frequently Asked Questions

## General

### What is this project?
This project helps developers solve [core problem] by providing [key benefits]
with minimal configuration.

## Installation & Setup

### Why do I get command not found after installing?
Make sure the installation directory is in your PATH:
```bash
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"
```

### How do I install a specific version?
```bash
pip install project-name==1.2.3
```

## Configuration

### How do I set environment variables?
Create a `.env` file in your project root with your variables.

## Troubleshooting

### Why does the server crash on startup?
Check: environment variables are set, database is running, port is not in use.

### Why are my changes not reflected?
Try: restart the server, clear cache, ensure you have hot-reload enabled.

## Contributing

### How do I report a bug?
Open an issue using the Bug Report template with reproduction steps.

### Where can I get help?
- GitHub Discussions
- Community Discord/Slack
- Email: support@example.com

title: CONTRIBUTOR_GUIDE.md
description: Comprehensive guide for new contributors covering setup, workflow, and standards.
content:
# Contributor Guide

Welcome! We're thrilled you're interested in contributing to this project.

## Quick Navigation
- Code of Conduct
- Getting Started
- Development Workflow
- Coding Standards
- Testing
- Pull Request Process

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites
- Python 3.10+ or Node.js 18+
- Git
- A GitHub account

### Setup
```bash
git clone https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/REPO.git
cd REPO
git remote add upstream https://github.com/ORIGINAL-OWNER/REPO.git
python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
```

### Development Workflow
1. Find an issue labeled good-first-issue
2. Create a feature branch
3. Make changes and commit
4. Push and open a PR

### Commit Message Convention
We follow Conventional Commits:
```
<type>(<scope>): <description>
```
Types: feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, test, chore, perf

## Coding Standards
- Follow PEP 8 (Python) or ESLint (JavaScript)
- Use type hints for all function signatures
- Write clean, self-documenting code

## Testing
- All new features must include tests
- Run the full test suite before submitting
```bash
pytest
```

## Pull Request Process
1. Describe the change clearly
2. Link to the issue (Fixes #123)
3. Keep PRs small and focused
4. Ensure CI passes before merging

title: ARCHITECTURE.md
description: System architecture documentation covering project structure, data flow, and design decisions.
content:
# Architecture Overview

## System Overview

The project follows a layered architecture:

```
+------------------+
|   Presentation   |  API endpoints, CLI, Web UI
+------------------+
+------------------+
|   Application    |  Use cases, business logic
+------------------+
+------------------+
|     Domain       |  Core entities, value objects
+------------------+
+------------------+
|  Infrastructure  |  Database, external APIs, file system
+------------------+
```

## Project Structure
```
project/
  src/
    presentation/   HTTP handlers, CLI commands
    application/    Use cases, services
    domain/         Core entities, business rules
    infrastructure/ Database models, external APIs
  tests/
    unit/           Unit tests
    integration/    Integration tests
  docs/             Documentation
```

## Technology Stack
| Component | Technology |
|-----------|------------|
| Language | Python 3.12 |
| Web Framework | FastAPI/Django |
| Database | PostgreSQL 16 |
| Cache | Redis 7 |
| Search | Elasticsearch 8 |

## Data Flow
1. Client sends request
2. Middleware processes (auth, rate limiting, logging)
3. Controller routes to appropriate service
4. Service executes business logic
5. Repository interacts with database
6. Response returned to client

## Security
- JWT tokens with short expiry
- Password hashing with bcrypt
- Rate limiting: 100 req/min per user
- CORS restricted to allowed origins
- SQL injection prevention via parameterized queries

title: RELEASE_NOTES.md
description: Structured release notes template for announcing new versions with highlights and changelog.
content:
# Release Notes

## Version 1.1.0

**Release Date**: 2026-04-15

### Highlights

This release introduces the user preference center, improves API performance
by 40%, and includes several bug fixes.

### New Features
- **User Preference Center**: Users can customize theme and notifications
- **Rate Limiting**: Public API endpoints now enforce rate limits
- **Health Check Endpoint**: New /health endpoint for system status

### Improvements
- Optimized dashboard query performance (40% faster)
- Updated UI components to match latest design system
- Improved error messages for API validation

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed CSRF token regeneration on page refresh
- Resolved timezone handling in scheduled tasks
- Fixed pagination offset when filtering by date

### Deprecations
- v1/users endpoint deprecated, use v2/users
- Python 3.8 support ending in v1.2.0

### Security
- Patched stored XSS vulnerability in profile bio
- Updated dependencies for CVE-2026-1234
- Added Content-Security-Policy header

title: CONTRIBUTING.md
description: Contribution guidelines covering Git workflow, branch strategy, and PR expectations.
content:
# Contributing to This Project

First off, thank you for considering contributing! We welcome contributions
of all kinds: bug fixes, features, documentation, tests, and design.

## Code of Conduct

This project adheres to a Code of Conduct. By participating, you agree to
uphold its standards of respectful and professional behavior.

## Getting Started

1. Fork the repository on GitHub
2. Clone your fork: `git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/REPO.git`
3. Add upstream: `git remote add upstream https://github.com/ORIGINAL_OWNER/REPO.git`

## Branch Strategy

- `main` - Production-ready code
- `develop` - Integration branch
- `feature/*` - New features
- `fix/*` - Bug fixes
- `hotfix/*` - Urgent production fixes

### Workflow
1. Create branch from develop: `git checkout -b feature/my-feature develop`
2. Commit following Conventional Commits
3. Push and open PR against develop
4. After review, squash-merge into develop

## Commit Guidelines

Follow Conventional Commits:
```
<type>(<scope>): <description>

feat(auth): add OAuth2 login with Google provider
fix(api): handle null response in user query
```

## Pull Request Process
1. Title uses conventional commit format
2. Description explains what and why
3. Include Fixes #123 or Closes #456
4. Keep PRs under 400 lines
5. Ensure all CI checks pass

title: Development Setup Guide
description: Step-by-step local development environment setup for new contributors.
content:
# Development Setup Guide

## Prerequisites
- Python 3.10+ (`python --version`)
- Node.js 18+ (`node --version`)
- Git (`git --version`)
- PostgreSQL 14+ or SQLite

## Step 1: Clone the Repository
```bash
git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/REPO.git
cd REPO
git remote add upstream https://github.com/ORIGINAL_OWNER/REPO.git
```

## Step 2: Set Up Python Virtual Environment
```bash
python3 -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate  # On Windows: venv\Scripts\activate
```

## Step 3: Install Dependencies
```bash
pip install --upgrade pip
pip install -r requirements.txt
pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
pre-commit install
```

## Step 4: Configure Environment
```bash
cp .env.example .env
# Edit .env with your local settings
```

## Step 5: Set Up the Database
```bash
python manage.py migrate
python manage.py seed_data
```

## Step 6: Verify Your Setup
```bash
pytest
python manage.py runserver
```
Visit http://127.0.0.1:8000/ in your browser.

## Common Issues

### Port 8000 already in use
```bash
python manage.py runserver 8080
```

### Database connection refused
Ensure PostgreSQL is running: `sudo systemctl start postgresql`

title: Coding Standards
description: Language-specific coding conventions enforced through automated linters and reviews.
content:
# Coding Standards

Consistent code style makes the codebase easier to read, review, and maintain.

## Python Standards

### Formatting
- Tool: Black with default settings
- Line length: 88 characters
- Quotes: Double quotes for strings

### Import Ordering
- Tool: isort
- Order: standard library, third-party, local

### Type Hints
- All function signatures must include type hints
- Use Optional[Type] for broader compatibility

### Docstrings
- Use Google-style docstrings
- Required for all public modules, classes, and functions
- Include Args, Returns, Raises sections

### Naming Conventions
| Element | Convention |
|---------|------------|
| Variables | snake_case |
| Functions | snake_case |
| Classes | PascalCase |
| Constants | UPPER_CASE |

### Code Quality Tools
```bash
black --check src/
isort --check-only src/
flake8 src/
mypy src/
pytest
```

## JavaScript / TypeScript Standards
### Formatting
- Tool: Prettier
- Semicolons: Required
- Quotes: Single quotes
- Print width: 100

### Naming Conventions
| Element | Convention |
|---------|------------|
| Variables | camelCase |
| Functions | camelCase |
| Classes | PascalCase |
| Files | kebab-case |

## HTML / CSS Standards
- Use semantic HTML5 elements
- CSS class naming: BEM methodology
- Responsive design: mobile-first approach

## Enforcement
These standards are enforced through:
1. Pre-commit hooks
2. CI pipeline
3. Code review

title: Branch Strategy
description: Git branching model and naming conventions for consistent collaboration.
content:
# Branch Strategy

We use a simplified Git Flow model adapted for continuous delivery.

## Branch Types

### main (Production)
- Always reflects the latest production-ready state
- Protected: direct pushes are prohibited
- Tags applied here for versioned releases

### develop (Integration)
- Default branch for development work
- Feature branches are merged here
- Must always pass CI checks

### feature/* (New Features)
- Naming: feature/<issue-number>-<description>
- Branched from: develop
- Merged into: develop

### fix/* (Bug Fixes)
- Naming: fix/<issue-number>-<description>
- Branched from: develop
- Merged into: develop

### hotfix/* (Urgent Production Fixes)
- Naming: hotfix/<issue-number>-<description>
- Branched from: main
- Merged into: main AND develop

### release/* (Release Preparation)
- Naming: release/<version>
- Branched from: develop
- Merged into: main AND develop

## Workflow Example
```bash
git checkout develop
git checkout -b fix/87-login-button-disabled
# ... make changes ...
git commit -m "fix(login): enable disabled button"
git push origin fix/87-login-button-disabled
# Create PR against develop
```

## Rules
1. Never commit directly to main or develop
2. Always create a PR for your changes
3. Keep PRs small (under 400 lines)
4. Squash commits when merging into develop
5. Delete branches after merging

title: Bug Report Template
description: Structured bug report form for consistent and actionable issue submissions.
content:
---
name: Bug Report
about: Create a report to help us improve
title: "[BUG]: "
labels: bug
assignees: ''
---

## Bug Description

A clear and concise description of what the bug is.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Go to '...'
2. Click on '....'
3. Scroll down to '....'
4. See error

## Expected Behavior

A clear description of what you expected to happen.

## Actual Behavior

A clear description of what actually happened, including any error messages.

## Environment
- OS: [e.g., macOS 14.5, Ubuntu 22.04, Windows 11]
- Browser: [e.g., Chrome 124, Firefox 125]
- Version: [e.g., v1.1.0]

## Screenshots

If applicable, add screenshots to help explain your problem.

## Additional Context
- Is this consistent or intermittent?
- Does it affect all users or a subset?
- Any recent changes that might be related?

## Checklist
- [ ] I have searched for existing issues before filing this report
- [ ] I have included all relevant environment information

title: Feature Request Template
description: Feature request form for proposing new functionality with detailed use cases.
content:
---
name: Feature Request
about: Suggest an idea for this project
title: "[FEATURE]: "
labels: enhancement
assignees: ''
---

## Problem Statement

Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.

## Proposed Solution

A clear and concise description of what you want to happen.

## Alternative Approaches

Describe alternatives you've considered. Why is your solution better?

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Users can do X in under 3 clicks
- [ ] Feature is discoverable without documentation
- [ ] Performance impact is under 50ms

## Target Users

Who will benefit from this feature?

## Use Cases

As a [role], I want [goal], so that [reason].

## Mockups / Designs (optional)

If you have wireframes or mockups, please attach them.

## Additional Context

Add any other context, links, or references.

---

Thank you for taking the time to suggest this improvement!

title: Documentation Issue Template
description: Report documentation gaps, errors, or improvement suggestions.
content:
---
name: Documentation Issue
about: Report a documentation error, gap, or improvement suggestion
title: "[DOCS]: "
labels: documentation
assignees: ''
---

## Issue Type

- [ ] Typo/Grammar: Spelling or grammatical error
- [ ] Inaccuracy: Information is outdated or incorrect
- [ ] Missing: Content that should exist but is absent
- [ ] Clarity: Content is confusing or unclear
- [ ] Broken Link: Link that doesn't work

## Location

Page: [URL or file path]
Section: [Specific heading]

## Current State

What does the documentation currently say?

## Expected State

What should it say instead?

## Impact

Who is affected by this issue?
- New users may struggle without correct documentation
- Contributors need accurate docs to contribute effectively

## Checklist
- [ ] I have searched for existing documentation issues
- [ ] I have provided the exact location of the issue

title: Performance Issue Template
description: Report performance bottlenecks, slowdowns, or scalability concerns.
content:
---
name: Performance Issue
about: Report a performance problem or scalability concern
title: "[PERF]: "
labels: performance
assignees: ''
---

## Performance Description

Describe the performance issue and how it affects the user experience.

## Measured Metrics

| Metric | Expected | Actual |
|--------|----------|--------|
| Page load time | < 2s | 8s |
| API response time | < 100ms | 850ms |
| Memory usage | < 256MB | 890MB |

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Navigate to '...'
2. Observe it taking X seconds

## Environment
- Environment: [Production / Staging / Local]
- Data volume: [e.g., 50K users]

## Investigation Done
- [ ] Checked browser DevTools
- [ ] Reviewed server logs
- [ ] Analyzed database query plans

## Suggested Fix (optional)
- Add database index on [column]
- Implement caching for [endpoint]
- Add pagination to [list view]

title: Security Report Template
description: Private vulnerability disclosure form for responsible security reporting.
content:
---
name: Security Report
about: Privately report a security vulnerability
title: "[SECURITY]: "
labels: security
assignees: ''
---

## Important Notice

PLEASE DO NOT DISCLOSE SECURITY ISSUES PUBLICLY. Send reports via email to
security@example.com or use GitHub private vulnerability reporting.

## Vulnerability Summary

A clear description without leaking exploitable details.

## Affected Components
- Project: [Component or module]
- Version(s): [Affected versions]

## Proof of Concept

Provide minimal reproduction steps.

## Impact Assessment
- Severity: [Critical / High / Medium / Low]
- Attack Vector: [Network / Local]
- Privileges Required: [None / Low / High]

## Remediation Suggestions

If you have ideas for fixing the vulnerability, describe them.

## Checklist
- [ ] I have not publicly disclosed this vulnerability
- [ ] I will send this report through the designated secure channel

title: Question Template
description: Template for asking usage questions, seeking clarification, or requesting guidance.
content:
---
name: Question
about: Ask a question about using this project
title: "[QUESTION]: "
labels: question
assignees: ''
---

## Question

What would you like to know?

## Context

What are you trying to achieve?

## What I've Tried

- [ ] Checked the README and documentation
- [ ] Searched existing issues
- [ ] Searched Stack Overflow

## Code Snippet (if applicable)

```python
# Paste relevant code here
```

## Environment
- Version: [e.g., v1.1.0]
- OS: [e.g., Ubuntu 22.04]

## Checklist
- [ ] I have searched for existing questions before posting
- [ ] I have provided enough context to understand my question

title: Enhancement Template
description: Propose improvements to existing features, UI, or developer experience.
content:
---
name: Enhancement
about: Suggest an improvement to an existing feature
title: "[ENHANCE]: "
labels: enhancement
assignees: ''
---

## Current State

Describe what currently exists and what is suboptimal about it.

## Desired Improvement

Describe how you envision the improvement.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Response time improves by 2x
- [ ] Users require one fewer click
- [ ] New contributors can onboard in 5 minutes

## User Stories

As a [type of user], I want [action], so that [benefit].

## Implementation Ideas (optional)

If you have technical suggestions on how to implement this.

## Impact Assessment
- Effort: [Small / Medium / Large]
- Risk: [Low / Medium / High]
- User Impact: [Low / Medium / High]

## Additional Context

Add any other context, screenshots, or examples.

title: Refactoring Request Template
description: Propose codebase restructuring, technical debt reduction, or pattern improvements.
content:
---
name: Refactoring Request
about: Propose refactoring to reduce technical debt or improve code quality
title: "[REFACTOR]: "
labels: refactoring
assignees: ''
---

## Current Code State

Describe the code that needs refactoring. Include file paths and modules.

## Pain Points

- [ ] Duplication: Same logic in multiple places
- [ ] Complexity: Cyclomatic complexity above threshold
- [ ] Testability: Difficult to write unit tests
- [ ] Performance: Specific performance issue
- [ ] Maintainability: Hard to understand or modify

## Proposed Refactoring

- [ ] Extract class/function into separate module
- [ ] Replace pattern with alternative
- [ ] Introduce design pattern
- [ ] Add type hints to module

## Benefits
- Readability: How will it be easier to understand?
- Testability: How will testing improve?
- Maintainability: How will future changes be easier?

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Code complexity metrics improved
- [ ] Test coverage maintained or improved
- [ ] All existing tests pass

title: Standard Pull Request Template
description: Standard PR checklist ensuring tests, docs, and code quality are verified before merging.
content:
## Related Issue

Fixes #(issue number)

## Summary of Changes

Brief summary of what this PR accomplishes.
- Added X to handle Y
- Updated Z for performance

## Type of Change
- [ ] Bug fix (non-breaking)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Refactoring (no functional changes)
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Performance improvement

## Checklist

### Code Quality
- [ ] Code follows coding standards
- [ ] Self-review completed
- [ ] Comments added in complex areas
- [ ] Type hints added

### Testing
- [ ] Tests added proving fix/feature works
- [ ] All existing tests pass

### Documentation
- [ ] README or docs updated
- [ ] CHANGELOG updated

## Screenshots (if UI change)

## Testing Instructions
1. Pull branch
2. Run migrations
3. Verify expected behavior

## Additional Context
- Blocked by PR #[number]
- Related to PR #[number]

title: Feature Pull Request Template
description: PR template for new feature contributions with implementation and testing details.
content:
## New Feature: [Feature Name]

## Feature Summary

Describe the feature being added.

## Related Issues
- Closes #(feature request issue)
- Depends on #(blocker issue)

## Implementation Details

### Architecture Changes
- New module: src/services/feature_service.py
- New endpoint: POST /api/v2/resource
- Modified: src/models/model.py (added 2 fields)

### Database Changes
- [ ] New migration: 0008_add_feature_fields.py
- [ ] New table: feature_table

### Configuration Changes
- [ ] New environment variable: FEATURE_FLAG

## Testing

### Unit Tests
- [ ] Tests added for service layer
- [ ] Tests added for API endpoints

### Manual Testing
- [ ] Verified feature works in development environment

## Documentation
- [ ] README updated
- [ ] CHANGELOG updated

## Feedback Requested
Specific areas where I'd like reviewer input:

title: Bug Fix Pull Request Template
description: PR template for bug fix contributions with reproduction and verification details.
content:
## Bug Fix: [Brief Description]

## Bug Reference
- Fixes #(bug report issue)
- Regression introduced in: PR #[number]

## Root Cause Analysis

Describe the root cause at the code level.

## The Fix

Explain what changes were made:
1. Added null-check for user in views/profile.py
2. Added fallback redirect to login page
3. Added unit test for the scenario

## Verification

### Before Fix
1. Set session timeout to 1 second
2. Navigate to /profile/
3. Observe 500 error

### After Fix
1. Same steps
2. Observe redirect to login page

## Automated Tests
- [ ] Added regression test
- [ ] Test fails before fix, passes after fix

## Files Changed
- src/app/views/profile.py - Added null check
- tests/test_profile.py - Added regression test

## Risk Assessment
- Impact: Low
- Backward Compatibility: Fully compatible

title: Documentation Pull Request Template
description: PR template for documentation updates, corrections, and additions.
content:
## Documentation Update

## Summary

Describe what documentation was changed and why.
- Fixed typos in README installation section
- Added API reference for new v2 endpoints
- Updated CONTRIBUTING.md with new branch strategy

## Files Changed
- [ ] README.md
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md
- [ ] CHANGELOG.md

## Quality Checklist
- [ ] Spelling and grammar checked
- [ ] Code examples are tested and working
- [ ] Links are valid
- [ ] Consistent terminology used

## Related Issues
- Closes #(documentation issue)

## Reviewer Notes
- Pay special attention to the [section] for accuracy
- All external URLs verified as of [date]

title: Release Pull Request Template
description: PR template for preparing and coordinating a new version release.
content:
## Release: v[VERSION]

## Release Summary
- Version: v[VERSION]
- Type: [Major / Minor / Patch]

## Pre-Release Checklist

### Code Preparation
- [ ] All features for this release merged to develop
- [ ] All bug fixes merged
- [ ] CI checks pass on develop

### Release Branch
- [ ] Release branch created: release/v[VERSION]
- [ ] Version bumped in all config files
- [ ] CHANGELOG.md finalized

### Testing
- [ ] Full test suite passes
- [ ] Smoke tests pass in staging
- [ ] Regression tests pass

### Documentation
- [ ] README updated if needed
- [ ] API documentation up to date
- [ ] Release notes drafted

## Release Steps
1. Merge release into main with --no-ff
2. Tag: git tag -a v[VERSION]
3. Deploy to production
4. Merge back into develop
5. Announce release

title: Hotfix Pull Request Template
description: Urgent production bug fix PR template with accelerated review checklist.
content:
## Hotfix: [Brief Description]

## Urgency
- Severity: [Critical / High]
- Affected Users: [Count or percentage]

## Bug Reference
- Fixes #(issue number)

## Root Cause

Brief description of the root cause.

## The Fix

Describe the minimal fix applied.

## Verification
- [ ] Bug reproduced in production-like environment
- [ ] Fix verified
- [ ] Existing tests pass

## Checklist
- [ ] Code reviewed by at least one maintainer
- [ ] Fix is minimal and targeted
- [ ] Follow-up issue created for permanent fix
- [ ] Hotfix deployed to production

## Merge Instructions
1. Merge into main
2. Deploy immediately
3. Merge main back into develop

title: Dependabot Configuration
description: GitHub Dependabot YAML configuration for automated dependency updates.
content:
# Dependabot Configuration
# Place in: .github/dependabot.yml

version: 2
updates:
  - package-ecosystem: "pip"
    directory: "/"
    schedule:
      interval: "weekly"
      day: "monday"
    open-pull-requests-limit: 10
    labels:
      - "dependencies"
      - "python"

  - package-ecosystem: "npm"
    directory: "/frontend"
    schedule:
      interval: "weekly"
      day: "monday"
    open-pull-requests-limit: 10
    labels:
      - "dependencies"
      - "javascript"

  - package-ecosystem: "github-actions"
    directory: "/"
    schedule:
      interval: "weekly"
    labels:
      - "dependencies"
      - "ci"

  - package-ecosystem: "docker"
    directory: "/"
    schedule:
      interval: "monthly"
    labels:
      - "dependencies"
      - "docker"

title: GitHub Actions CI Workflow
description: CI workflow for automated testing, linting, and type checking on every push.
content:
# CI Workflow
# Place in: .github/workflows/ci.yml

name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, develop]

jobs:
  lint:
    name: Lint & Type Check
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"
          cache: "pip"
      - run: pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
      - run: flake8 src/ --max-line-length=88
      - run: black --check src/
      - run: mypy src/

  test:
    name: Run Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:16
        env:
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres
          POSTGRES_DB: test_db
        options: --health-cmd pg_isready --health-interval 10s
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"
          cache: "pip"
      - run: pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
      - run: pytest --cov=src --cov-report=xml
      - uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4

title: Node.js Workflow
description: GitHub Actions workflow for Node.js projects with testing and linting.
content:
# Node.js CI Workflow
# Place in: .github/workflows/nodejs.yml

name: Node.js CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
    paths:
      - "frontend/**"
      - "package.json"
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, develop]

jobs:
  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"
          cache: "yarn"
      - run: yarn install --frozen-lockfile
      - run: yarn lint

  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"
          cache: "yarn"
      - run: yarn install --frozen-lockfile
      - run: yarn test --coverage

  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: "20"
          cache: "yarn"
      - run: yarn install --frozen-lockfile
      - run: yarn build

title: Java Maven Workflow
description: GitHub Actions workflow for Java projects using Maven build system.
content:
# Java Maven CI Workflow
# Place in: .github/workflows/maven.yml

name: Java Maven CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, develop]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: "21"
          distribution: "temurin"
          cache: maven
      - run: mvn --batch-mode verify
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: always()
        with:
          name: test-results
          path: target/surefire-reports/

  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: "21"
          distribution: "temurin"
          cache: maven
      - run: mvn checkstyle:check
      - run: mvn pmd:check

title: Spring Boot Workflow
description: GitHub Actions workflow for Spring Boot applications with Docker build.
content:
# Spring Boot CI/CD Workflow
# Place in: .github/workflows/spring-boot.yml

name: Spring Boot CI/CD

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
  release:
    types: [published]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:16
        env:
          POSTGRES_DB: testdb
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: test
        options: --health-cmd pg_isready
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: "21"
          distribution: "temurin"
          cache: maven
      - run: mvn --batch-mode test

  docker:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [test]
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: "21"
          distribution: "temurin"
          cache: maven
      - run: mvn --batch-mode package -DskipTests
      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
      - uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v6
        with:
          context: .
          push: true
          tags: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}:latest

title: Docker Build Workflow
description: GitHub Actions workflow for building and pushing Docker images with multi-arch support.
content:
# Docker Build Workflow
# Place in: .github/workflows/docker.yml

name: Docker Build

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    tags: ["v*"]

env:
  REGISTRY: ghcr.io
  IMAGE_NAME: ${{ github.repository }}

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write
      id-token: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: docker/setup-qemu-action@v3
      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
      - uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      - uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        id: meta
        with:
          images: ${{ env.REGISTRY }}/${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }}
          tags: |
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable={{is_default_branch}}
      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v6
        with:
          context: .
          platforms: linux/amd64,linux/arm64
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}

title: Release Workflow
description: GitHub Actions workflow for automated version releases with changelog generation.
content:
# Release Workflow
# Place in: .github/workflows/release.yml

name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags: ["v*"]

permissions:
  contents: write
  packages: write

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          generate_release_notes: true
          files: |
            dist/*.whl
            dist/*.tar.gz

  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [release]
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: "3.12"
      - run: pip install -r requirements.txt
      - run: python -m build
      - env:
          TWINE_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.PYPI_API_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          pip install twine
          twine upload dist/*

title: Bug Report (Issue Form YAML)
description: GitHub Issue Form (YAML) for structured bug reports with form inputs and validation.
content:
name: Bug Report
description: File a bug report to help us improve
title: "[BUG]: "
labels: ["bug", "needs-triage"]
body:
  - type: markdown
    attributes:
      value: Thanks for filing a bug report!
  - type: input
    id: version
    attributes:
      label: Version
      placeholder: v1.2.3
    validations:
      required: true
  - type: textarea
    id: what-happened
    attributes:
      label: What happened?
      placeholder: When I click X, Y happens instead of Z
    validations:
      required: true
  - type: textarea
    id: reproduction
    attributes:
      label: Steps to Reproduce
      placeholder: |
        1. Go to ...
        2. Click on ...
        3. See error
    validations:
      required: true
  - type: textarea
    id: expected
    attributes:
      label: Expected Behavior
    validations:
      required: true
  - type: dropdown
    id: os
    attributes:
      label: OS
      options:
        - macOS
        - Linux
        - Windows
  - type: checkboxes
    id: terms
    attributes:
      label: Checklist
      options:
        - label: I have searched existing issues
          required: true

title: Feature Request (Issue Form YAML)
description: GitHub Issue Form (YAML) for structured feature requests with form validation.
content:
name: Feature Request
description: Suggest an idea for this project
title: "[FEATURE]: "
labels: ["enhancement"]
body:
  - type: markdown
    attributes:
      value: Thanks for suggesting a feature!
  - type: textarea
    id: problem
    attributes:
      label: Problem Statement
      placeholder: I'm always frustrated when...
    validations:
      required: true
  - type: textarea
    id: solution
    attributes:
      label: Proposed Solution
      placeholder: I'd like to see...
    validations:
      required: true
  - type: textarea
    id: alternatives
    attributes:
      label: Alternative Approaches
  - type: dropdown
    id: priority
    attributes:
      label: Priority
      options:
        - Nice to have
        - Important
        - Critical
  - type: checkboxes
    id: terms
    attributes:
      label: Checklist
      options:
        - label: I have searched existing feature requests
          required: true

title: Question (Issue Form YAML)
description: GitHub Issue Form (YAML) for structured questions and help requests.
content:
name: Question
description: Ask a question about using this project
title: "[QUESTION]: "
labels: ["question"]
body:
  - type: markdown
    attributes:
      value: Have a question? We're here to help!
  - type: input
    id: version
    attributes:
      label: Version
      placeholder: v1.2.3
  - type: textarea
    id: question
    attributes:
      label: Your Question
      placeholder: How do I configure...
    validations:
      required: true
  - type: textarea
    id: what-tried
    attributes:
      label: What I've Tried
  - type: textarea
    id: code
    attributes:
      label: Code Snippet
      render: python
  - type: checkboxes
    id: terms
    attributes:
      label: Checklist
      options:
        - label: I have searched existing questions
          required: true

title: Documentation (Issue Form YAML)
description: GitHub Issue Form (YAML) for documentation gap reports and improvement suggestions.
content:
name: Documentation Issue
description: Report a documentation gap or suggest improvement
title: "[DOCS]: "
labels: ["documentation"]
body:
  - type: dropdown
    id: type
    attributes:
      label: Issue Type
      options:
        - Typo or grammatical error
        - Incorrect or outdated information
        - Missing documentation
        - Broken link
    validations:
      required: true
  - type: input
    id: location
    attributes:
      label: Location
      placeholder: "README.md, Installation section"
    validations:
      required: true
  - type: textarea
    id: current
    attributes:
      label: Current State
  - type: textarea
    id: expected
    attributes:
      label: Expected State
    validations:
      required: true
  - type: checkboxes
    id: terms
    attributes:
      label: Checklist
      options:
        - label: I have searched existing documentation issues
          required: true

title: GitHub Discussions - Ideas
description: GitHub Discussions category template for sharing and discussing new ideas.
content:
---
labels: ["ideas", "discussion"]
---

## Idea: [Title]

### Summary

Briefly describe your idea in one or two sentences.

### Problem It Solves

What problem does this idea solve?

### Proposed Solution

Describe your idea in detail from a user's perspective.

### Benefits
- Benefit 1
- Benefit 2
- Benefit 3

### Potential Challenges
- Challenge 1
- Challenge 2

### Prior Art

Are there existing projects that do something similar?

### Questions for Discussion
1. Question for the community
2. Question for the community

---

Thanks for sharing your idea!

title: GitHub Discussions - Q&A
description: GitHub Discussions category template for asking questions and getting help.
content:
---
labels: ["question", "help-wanted"]
---

### What I'm Trying To Do

Describe your goal.

### What I've Tried

List the steps you've already taken.

### What's Not Working

Describe the problem.

### Environment
- Version: [e.g., v1.2.0]
- OS: [e.g., macOS 14.5]
- Language Version: [e.g., Python 3.12]

### Code / Configuration

```python
# Share relevant code
```

### What I Expected

### What I've Researched

- [ ] Searched existing discussions

title: GitHub Discussions - Show and Tell
description: GitHub Discussions template for sharing what you've built with the project.
content:
---
labels: ["show-and-tell", "community"]
---

## Show and Tell: [Project Name]

### What I Built

Describe your project or what you created.

### Links
- Repository: [GitHub URL]
- Live Demo: [URL]

### How I Used This Project

Explain how this project was useful.

### What I Learned

Share any insights or tips.

### Challenges I Faced

What was difficult and how did you overcome it?

### Screenshots / Demo

### Tech Stack Used
- Technology 1
- Technology 2

### Would You Recommend This Project?

[Yes/No] - Why?

---

We love seeing what the community builds!
